*********************************************************************** THIS DOCUMENT IS OUT OF DATE AND IS HERE FOR HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY bSpace was replaced by http://bcourses.berkeley.edu in October 2015. **********************************

n October 2015. ***************************************************************************** July 15 2013 CONTENTS bSpace Best Practices How to archive course site contents from a previous semester? How do visiting lecturers ge

course site? How do I add a non-UCB guest account to a bSpace course site? Can you email a posting to a Forum (formerly "Discussion")? Can Forum be a recipient of an Email Archive? Do class sites or selected content expire at the end of a semeste

or selected content expire at the end of a semester? Do project sites expire? Can a class have a permanent Wiki? (use a project site?) How do I set and find a URL to that is world-wide accessible? What tools are only in a class or project site?

What tools are only in a class or project site? Is there a limit to how much content I can upload to my bSpace site? Where are the assignment submissions stored? date stamps, audit trail? Is it possible to archive the entire contents of the Forum

ssible to archive the entire contents of the Forum? Do Guest or Non-UC Berkeley Participant accounts expire automatically? Can the ownership of a site be transfered to a new user? Can a delegate be assigned to a project site? Can a delegate be au

assigned to a project site? Can a delegate be automatically assigned to all courses in a department? How can an instructor restrict access the course site? bSpace Best Practices --------------------- Please see http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/

----- Please see http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/setup.html#class_page for a summary of the course WEB servers that are available for EECS courses We recommend that instructors use the EECS course WEB site for content that should be public,

rse WEB site for content that should be public, permanent and archived, and that they use bSpace (below) for on-line communication with the current group of students. bSpace includes password-protected tools for email, wiki, discussion, a

d-protected tools for email, wiki, discussion, announcements, chat, class section and grade management. bSpace lacks features for convenient archiving and automated management. It is not possible to archive the Email and Forum content to an

ible to archive the Email and Forum content to an off-site location. The site retention policy is subject to change. So, do not assume that the data in a bSpace course site will be retained after the current semester. July 2013: bSpace w

after the current semester. July 2013: bSpace was updated to Sakai 2.9. There is a 2-year plan to migrate to a new LMS (http://ets.berkeley.edu/bspace-replacement). How to archive course site contents from a previous semester? --------------

contents from a previous semester? ------------------------------------------------------------- The instructor and the department delegate's role can download selected content. For a list of the bSpace roles and permissions, see https://bs

the bSpace roles and permissions, see https://bspace.berkeley.edu/access/content/public/role_permissions_course.pdf There is no export ability with the Forum tool. It is not possible to archive the entire contents of the Forum, perhaps using

ve the entire contents of the Forum, perhaps using WebDEV or scripting. How do visiting lecturers get assigned to a bSpace course site? --------------------------------------------------------------- See http://bspacehelp.berkeley.edu/faq/i-am

--- See http://bspacehelp.berkeley.edu/faq/i-am-a-visiting-lecturer-do-i-need-a-guest-account. bSpace automatically associates a course WEB site with the instructor who is in the Registrar's records. The department scheduler must notify the

ecords. The department scheduler must notify the Registar if the visiting lecturer is the instructor of record. How do Concurrent students access a bSpace course site? ------------------------------------------------------- Concurrent student

---------------------------- Concurrent students receive their ID's from OR [How? Via email? Postal mail? Don't know.] Once their enrollment petitions have been reviewed, approved, and processed by the College and OR. This usually does no

essed by the College and OR. This usually does not happen until a few weeks into the semester. Until this happens, they have been instructed to ask their professors for the temporary access password (see below). Also, from http://www.un

password (see below). Also, from http://www.unex.berkeley.edu/info/concurrent_faq.html#22: Once your current-term application has a status of "Approval Completed," you will be assigned an Extension ID and PIN. Your Extension ID an

an Extension ID and PIN. Your Extension ID and PIN are displayed on the Personal Information page of your concurrent enrollment application account. Your Extension ID is your CalNet ID. [How do they know when their status is approve

[How do they know when their status is approved and where do they get the Personal Information page? Don't know.] This program is different from the UC Extension concurrent enrollment (see below). How do UC Extension Concurrent student

ee below). How do UC Extension Concurrent students access a bSpace course site? -------------------------------------------------------------------- These students may have forms with information to contact staff in L&S Advisor's office. They

to contact staff in L&S Advisor's office. They are participating (Fall 2007) in the UC/CC Concurrent Enrollment program (a program for eligible community college students). How do I add a non-UCB guest account to a bSpace course site? ---

non-UCB guest account to a bSpace course site? ------------------------------------------------------------- This includes Concurrent enrollment students who have NOT yet been issued a CalNet account. Anyone can be added to a bSpace site! Fo

ount. Anyone can be added to a bSpace site! Follow these steps to add someone to your site who is NOT a student, faculty, or staff and UC Berkeley: 1) Login to bSpace and click on the tab of your course site 2) Click the Site info tool in

your course site 2) Click the Site info tool in your Course Tools menu 3) Click the Add Participants link in the Site Info menu 4) Enter the user's email address Non-UC Berkeley Participants box 5) Click the Continue button 6) Next, click

x 5) Click the Continue button 6) Next, click the Role radio button (typically student or guest) for the person you are adding and Click Continue 7) Decide whether you want to send them an email notification and click the Continue but

email notification and click the Continue button 8) Click the Finish button NOTE: UC Berkeley students, faculty, or staff who are auditing a class as a "guest" MUST be added with their @berkeley email address or UID as found in the ca

erkeley email address or UID as found in the campus directory (http://directory.berkeley.edu). Also see http://bspacehelp.berkeley.edu/faq/can-i-create-my-own-guest-accounts http://ets.berkeley.edu/TrainingSupportCenter/bSpace/How_To/bspace

ley.edu/TrainingSupportCenter/bSpace/How_To/bspace_add_non_uc_guests.htm Can you email a posting to a Forum (formerly "Discussion")? ----------------------------------------------------------- You can send email through the Email Archive tool wit

can send email through the Email Archive tool without logging in to bSpace first. (You have to login to use Announcements and Mailtool). The Email Archive tool creates a listserv in your site, allowing you to send email to a specific email

allowing you to send email to a specific email address that is then distributed to all the members of your site. Email sent through the Email Archive tool is archived and searchable within the bSpace site. It is not posted in the Home ar

e bSpace site. It is not posted in the Home area of the site. The sender must be an email address listed in the Email Archive tool. The reader must login to bSpace to read Email Archive. The Disucssion Tool as no "MARK AS READ" feature

The Disucssion Tool as no "MARK AS READ" feature to hide old messages, but it can reverse sort by Date Received. Can Forum be a recipient of an Email Archive? --------------------------------------------- Is it possible to email an article t

-------- Is it possible to email an article to a Forum without logging into bSpace, in the same way that one can email a message to an Email Archive? Perhaps it is possible to add a Forum as a recipient in Email Archive? No. Also, y

a recipient in Email Archive? No. Also, you can't receive email notification about Forum messages. Do class sites or selected content expire at the end of a semester? Do project sites expire? Can a class have a permanent Wiki? (use a projec

? Can a class have a permanent Wiki? (use a project site?) -------------------------------------------------------- How long are courses available to students after the term ends? As of Fall 2008, students will remain on the roster indefinitely.

, students will remain on the roster indefinitely. This allows instructors to view certain student-based data, such as grades, after a term has ended. Instructors can unpublish the site if they would like to block student access to the site,

ey would like to block student access to the site, or they can manually disassociate the roster from the site (Site Info-> "Edit Roster(s)"). Right now we are still developing our archiving policy for bSpace sites, though it will likely be th

licy for bSpace sites, though it will likely be three years. At this time project sites do not expire. In the future bSpace may implement a policy that archives project sites that are not actively used. It seems that an instructor could

tively used. It seems that an instructor could use a project site to host a permanent Wiki about a class. How do I set and find a URL to that is world-wide accessible? ------------------------------------------------------------- "publicly

------------------------------------- "publicly viewable" is an option for Announcements, Resources, and Syllabus. List the site "publicly viewable" in the bSpace searchable directory. It is accessed via Sites prior to logging in to bSpace.

ccessed via Sites prior to logging in to bSpace. What tools are only in a class or project site? ----------------------------------------------- Course sites are linked to the registrar's database. Course sites have Section Info to manage s

base. Course sites have Section Info to manage sections of the course. Roster Tool and Syllabus Tool are only available in Course Sites. Project sites have a button in Site Info labeled "Manage Groups" Project sites: Manage Groups work wit

ge Groups" Project sites: Manage Groups work with the Announcement tool Is there a limit to how much content I can upload to my bSpace site? -------------------------------------------------------------------- Each site has a total space limita

------------ Each site has a total space limitation of 1GB. There is no limitation on the number of bSpace sites that you create. When uploading files to the Resources tool, you can only upload 50MB at a time. To give you an idea, the ave

ad 50MB at a time. To give you an idea, the average pdf document is around 400 KB or equal to 0.00038 GB. However, if you upload many high-res video clips you may run into more space issues. Where are the assignment submissions stored? dat

Where are the assignment submissions stored? date stamps, audit trail? ---------------------------------------------------------------------- Some content can be copied from bSpace using WebDAV. To enable WebDAV, click the "Upload-Download Mu

To enable WebDAV, click the "Upload-Download Multiple Resources" button in the Resources tool, and follow the instructions. Is it possible to archive the entire contents of the Forum? -----------------------------------------------------------

-------------------------------------------------- Can a department delegate be allowed to delete all the postings in the Forum or unpublish access to a former course site? Or would bSpace staff be willing, on request, to unpublish access to f

f be willing, on request, to unpublish access to former course sites whose instructors have abandoned them? bSpcace does not recommend deleting all the postings. You can simply unpublish the site at the end of the semester to prevent student

site at the end of the semester to prevent student access if that is what you wish. That would be in the instructor's hands as to when and why they want to unpublish a site. It is done through Site Info->Manage Access. Do Guest or Non-UC

Site Info->Manage Access. Do Guest or Non-UC Berkeley Participant accounts expire automatically? ---------------------------------------------------------------------- No. At this time, there are no limitations on non-UC guests. Can th

re are no limitations on non-UC guests. Can the ownership of a site be transfered to a new user? -------------------------------------------------------- Some instructors would like permanent sites that can be tranfered to whomever is teach

ites that can be tranfered to whomever is teaching the course. Yes. You should make the new site owner have the role of maintain in project site and instructor in a course site. And then change the site contact info under Site Info->Edi

ange the site contact info under Site Info->Edit Site Information. Can a delegate be assigned to a project site? --------------------------------------------- Yes. And they can have any role. It is on the instructor to decide when it is

It is on the instructor to decide when it is appropriate to give them a more responsible/active role. Can a delegate be automatically assigned to all courses in a department? --------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------ There is not an automated way yet for bSpace to assign a delegate for everything in a given department. Each site owner has to add the delegates for that site. A delegate does not ge

delegates for that site. A delegate does not get full management of a course site How can an instructor restrict access the course site? ------------------------------------------------------ An instructor wishes to allow access the course

An instructor wishes to allow access the course site for currently enrolled students, but not for the outside world. You can control access to an entire site via the course site-> Site Info-> Manage Access page. If you check "Publish site"

> Manage Access page. If you check "Publish site" and not "Allow anyone with a bSpace account", then only the people you have added on the Site Info->Add Participants page can "join" and access the content. That seems to encompass the e

ess the content. That seems to encompass the entire site, but you could create a separate site (My Workspace->Workspace Tools->Worksite Setup-> New) and give that the restricted access. The benefit of bSpace is that it knows who is enro

benefit of bSpace is that it knows who is enrolled in the class and lets them login using their pre-existing Calnet IDs. EECS Instructional Support Group 386 Cory Hall, 333 Soda Hall inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
